translated: "Does it feel strange?"
"Yes."
"I'm so unused to seeing myself like this that I can't tell if it suits me."
"I see."
"But I've always wanted to wear something like this."
[CORNER: Story Watchalong] It must be so rewarding to make clothes that make people say that.
"Do you like cute clothes?"
"I do."
"I've always admired them."
"But since I was little, I never really owned anything cute."
"I didn't think they'd suit me, and I had this bias that I was more of a 'cool' type, so I couldn't admit I liked cute things."
"There was one time I worked up the courage to wear something like this."
"A cute dress just like this one."
"But then a friend told me, 'That's not like you at all. What happened?'"
No, that's not what you say!
You're supposed to say, "Oh, that's rare! It looks great on you!"
That's the correct answer.
Everyone, please be careful about that.
Saying things like...
"That's not like you."
People take that as a roundabout way of saying it doesn't suit them.
It's like her heart just broke right there, even though she looked great.
But it's not like you should just give empty praise if it really doesn't fit, either.
That's not what I mean.
I'm not saying you should lie just to be nice.
"I see."
"So that's what that look in your eyes meant."
Being told you shouldn't wear what you like because it's "not like you"... that "likeness" is just something others decide.
It's basically forcing a stereotype on someone.
"Even so..."
"This dress was so cute that I couldn't stop thinking about how it would feel to wear it."
"I understand."
"That's why I kept coming back to this shop."
A piece of clothing that makes you feel that way is wonderful.
"Thank you for coming back today."
"Huh?"
"Seeing you in that dress makes me so happy too."
"Because I got to see such a wonderful smile."
Oh!
"If you want to wear cute clothes, I think you should cherish that feeling."
What beautiful words.
That's basically the main theme of ProSeka, isn't it?
Those "feelings."
"I really do love cute things, and age doesn't matter..."
"...when it comes to your feelings."
"I don't want to lie to myself anymore."
Yeah.
"Um, thank you."
"Thanks to you, I realized what I want to cherish."
"I really love cute things, and I love this dress!" As a creator, hearing that would make me so happy.
To hear that as the person who made it.
"It suits you very well."
Ah...
I see.
"When I felt that the clothes I made gave someone a push forward, I felt this incredible emotion."
"This is..."
"...something that makes me so happy."
"It must be this exact moment."
"The shopkeeper..."
"...is amazing."
Yeah.
"She's truly radiant."
"I hope I can do something like this someday too."
Wonderful.
What a great story.
It's a truly beautiful story.
That's how I feel.
Since I've already chosen my own career path,
witnessing the moment someone else makes a choice—even if it's through an event story—is special.
There are always moments where you wonder if you've made the right choice, but if you can look back in 10 or 20 years and be glad you chose that path, then...
I think that's a good thing. But that usually requires effort, and I believe there's no right or wrong choice when you put in the work.
After a year or two, you might think, "Oh, I shouldn't have done this."
But looking back after 10 or 20 years,
I'm sure there are times when it all connects in the end.
You'll think, "If I hadn't done that then, I wouldn't be able to do this now."
Even if you're not in the same profession, I'm sure you'll feel that way.
So if there's something you're working hard at, or something you want to try, please keep at it.
I hope this event story serves as a catalyst for people to make those decisions for themselves.
